scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every practical GET list resource documented under /core and /time_off is a stream. The entire documented Zenefits API is read-only (no POST/PATCH/PUT/DELETE endpoint exists anywhere in the reference docs, confirmed via developers.zenefits.com/llms.txt's full page index and every individual reference page) so capabilities.write stays false and no writes.json is added -- this is a real API property, not a scope-narrowing choice. The /platform/* application-management surface (Applications, Company Installations, Flows) manages the caller's OWN marketplace-app registration/installation status and hiring-flow completion, not this connector's declared HR-data-read purpose (people/companies/departments and adjacent HR object data for one already-installed company), so it is excluded as out_of_scope; Events/Webhooks are inbound push subscriptions Zenefits calls into the caller's own server during app installation, not a pollable REST resource, excluded as non_data_endpoint.

excluded.reason: company-scoped variant of the same people list already emitted by the top-level people stream; the token is already scoped to one company so the top-level path returns the identical record set
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the people list stream
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the companies list stream
company-scoped variant of the same departments list already emitted by the top-level departments stream
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the departments list stream
company-scoped variant of the same locations list already emitted by the top-level locations stream
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the locations list stream
person-scoped variant of the same employments list already emitted by the top-level employments stream
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the employments list stream
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the custom_fields list stream
person-scoped variant of the same custom field value list already emitted by the top-level custom_field_values stream
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the custom_field_values list stream

company-scoped variant of the same company bank account list already emitted by the top-level company_banks stream (Zenefits' own docs note this is identical since access tokens are unique per company)
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the company_banks list stream
person-scoped variant of the same employee bank account list already emitted by the top-level employee_banks stream
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the employee_banks list stream
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the labor_group_types list stream
labor-group-type-scoped variant of the same labor groups list already emitted by the top-level labor_groups stream
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the labor_groups list stream
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the vacation_types list stream
vacation-type-scoped variant of the same vacation requests list already emitted by the top-level vacation_requests stream
detail-by-id view of a record already fully emitted by the vacation_requests list stream
returns metadata about the currently-authorized token (granted scopes, expiry, the token's own company/person) rather than a syncable object collection
Zenefits event delivery is an inbound push subscription configured during marketplace-app installation (Zenefits POSTs to a caller-hosted URL); there is no pollable REST resource to model as a stream
lists the caller's OWN registered marketplace application(s); irrelevant to this connector's declared purpose of reading one company's HR object data
lists which companies have installed the caller's marketplace app, and installation/subscription status; an app-management concern, not HR object data
hiring/onboarding/termination flow-completion status for the caller's own marketplace-app subscription model, not a general HR data object
